markdown:
**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 30 --- You vent a jet of supercooled fluid from your innovation, damaging a foe and slowing it down. Target one creature within 60 feet. That creature takes cold damage equal to your level, with effects depending on its Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage and takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage, is slowed 1 for 1 round, and takes a –15-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. **Unstable Function** Your innovation discharges an enormous cone of supercooled fluid, potentially causing cascading failures. Add the [unstable](/Traits.aspx?ID=416) trait to Deep Freeze. The ability affects all creatures within a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) instead of a single target, and it deals cold damage equal to double your level instead of equal to your level.
name: Deep Freeze

    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gigavolt](/Feats.aspx?ID=8578) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 31

**Prerequisites**
[Megavolt](/Feats.aspx?ID=8564)

</column>

---

When you unleash electricity from your innovation, you can bounce it off obstacles, creating a field of electric death. When you use Megavolt, each time your line of electricity hits a solid barrier, such as a wall, it bounces off at an angle of your choice and continues until you have reached the full length of the [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388). Even if a creature is in the area from multiple bounces, it takes the damage only once.
name: Gigavolt

<title

    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shared Overdrive](/Feats.aspx?ID=8579) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 31

**Prerequisites**
[Overdrive Ally](/Feats.aspx?ID=8569)

</column>

---

You’ve experimented enough on your teammates to transfer a substantial number of powered-up gizmos to them, enabling one of your allies to benefit from the full effects and duration of your Overdrive. The first time you use Overdrive Ally during a given Overdrive, the effect lasts for the remainder of the duration of your Overdrive, instead of just until the end of the target’s next turn. Any further uses of Overdrive Ally during the same Overdrive have their normal duration, per Overdrive Ally.

<title level="2">Shared Overdrive leads to...</title>
[Ubiquitous Overdrive](/Feats.aspx?ID=8591)
name: Shared Overdrive

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Explosive Maneuver](/Feats.aspx?ID=8580) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 31

**Prerequisites**
[weapon innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=7)

**Requirements**
Your last action was a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against a foe using your weapon innovation.

</column>

---

Thanks to a hidden experimental feature you built into your weapon, your weapon explosively deploys levers, tangling hooks, or similar mechanisms to provide significant assistance when you perform a combat maneuver. When you use Explosive Maneuver, choose [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382). Your weapon innovation must have a weapon trait that matches the action you chose (for instance, to choose [Grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619), your weapon must have the grapple trait). You take the chosen action against the same foe as your previous successful Strike, using the same multiple attack penalty as your previous successful Strike. You still increase your multiple attack penalty after the Grapple, Shove, or Trip, as normal.
name: Explosive Maneuver
requirement_markdown: Your last action was a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against a foe using your weapon innovation.

<traits>
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 32

**Trigger**
You would attempt the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) for an unstable action, but you haven’t rolled the flat check yet.

</column>

---

You’ve built triple redundancies into your innovation and added all sorts of buffers to protect it from the harm of your unstable experiments. You automatically succeed at the triggering flat check.

You can’t rely on your Unstable Redundancies again until you spend 10 minutes setting them back up. If you spend 10 minutes retuning your innovation so you can use [unstable](/Traits.aspx?ID=416) actions again, you can set up your Unstable Redundancies during the same time.
name: Unstable Redundancies

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Just the Thing!](/Feats.aspx?ID=8584) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 32

**Frequency**
once per hour

</column>

---

Need to balance on a razor’s edge, force open an iron door, or persuade a dragon to negotiate? Never fear! No matter the situation, you always have just the thing. You attempt a skill action that takes 1 minute or less to complete, using the same number of actions or amount of time as normal. However, as you take the action, describe a device you pull out and use to accomplish the skill. The specifics of how you accomplish this are up to you, but they should fit the challenge at hand. For instance, you might use gravitic stabilizers to balance on the razor’s edge, a force battering ram gizmo to open the iron door, or a device that produces an aroma with the ability to pacify wyrms to persuade the dragon. Using an invention in this way lets you alter how you calculate the skill check used in the action. Instead of the normal skill modifier associated with that skill action, you use your [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) modifier.
name: Just the Thing!

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[You Failed to Account for… This!](/Feats.aspx?ID=8586) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 32

**Trigger**
A creature you can see targets you with an attack against your AC.

</column>

---

When your foes try to attack you, you always seem to have some outlandish invention you can pull out at the last second to protect you from whatever attack they throw at you. Describe a device you’re attempting to use to protect yourself from the foe’s attack—for instance, when attacked by a [shocker lizard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=802)’s shock Strike, you might pull out a specially grounded lightning rod from that time you tried to power an invention by harnessing a thunderstorm! Using an invention to defend in this way means that the attack roll for the triggering attack targets your [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) DC instead of your AC.

Since you’re using your Crafting DC instead of your AC, any penalties to your AC don’t apply, but this doesn’t remove any conditions or other effects that are causing you such penalties. For instance, if you were [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) and used an invention to defend against a sneak attack, you’d still take the extra precision damage if you were hit, even though the –2 penalty to AC from being flat-footed wouldn’t apply to your Crafting DC.
name: You Failed to Account for… This!

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Devastating Weaponry](/Feats.aspx?ID=8587) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Inventor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=408" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 32

**Prerequisites**
[weapon innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=7)

</column>

---

You release a volley of projectiles from your weapon, or power it to fly in every direction across the battlefield and Strike multiple creatures. Make a Strike with your weapon innovation against each foe within 30 feet of you. Don’t increase your multiple attack penalty until after making all the attacks. If your innovation is a melee weapon and any of the attacks are outside your reach, you [Release](/Actions.aspx?ID=2300) the weapon before the Strikes, and it returns to your grasp after all of them. If your hands are full when the weapon returns, it falls to the ground in your space. As normal, if your weapon innovation requires one or more actions to [reload](/Rules.aspx?ID=2196) between Strikes, you can’t use it to make multiple attacks with Devastating Weaponry.
name: Devastating Weaponry

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 33 **Prerequisites** [armor](/Innovations.aspx?ID=5), [construct](/Innovations.aspx?ID=6), or [weapon innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=7) --- You become so entwined with your innovations that you can use them without a second thought. You’re permanently quickened. How you can use the extra action depends on your innovation.
  • **Armor** Your armor responds to the most subtle stimuli to move you in the right direction as long as you are wearing it. You can use the extra action to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), or use a form of movement provided by your innovation (such as [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381)).
  • **Construct** Your ability to command your construct becomes instinctive. You can use the extra action to [Command](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) your construct innovation (or to provide 1 of the actions if you choose to spend 2 actions to Command your construct).
  • **Weapon** Your weapon becomes easier to wield with deadly efficacy, almost as if it is an extension of your body. It speeds effortlessly toward whatever target you choose as long as you are holding it. You can use the extra action to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with your innovation.
name: Full Automation

---

You prepare a variety of incredible gizmos on each of your allies, linking them together through careful modification to enable you to power them up all at once. During your daily preparations, select up to six willing allies. Whenever you Overdrive, you grant the benefits of your overdrive to any of these allies you choose who are within 30 feet of you. The allies don’t gain the increased damage from expert, master, or legendary overdrive.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Automaton Armament](/Feats.aspx?ID=8592) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Automaton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=398" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 39

</column>

---

You’ve been provided a body part designed for combat. You gain either a claw or pincer unarmed attack. The claw deals 1d4 slashing damage, is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group, and has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits. The pincer deals 1d6 piercing damage, is in the brawling group, and has the [grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619) and unarmed traits.

Your body can be reconfigured; you can select this feat at any level, and you can retrain into or out of this feat or change the type of attack you gain.

**Enhancement** Your attacking part is reinforced. Increase the damage die of the unarmed attack you gain from this feat by one step (from 1d4 to 1d6, or from 1d6 to 1d8).
name: Automaton Armament
search_markdown:
<traits>
<trait label="Automaton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=398" />
</traits>

You can channel your core’s power through the magical gem that serves as your eye. You gain an energy beam ranged unarmed attack that deals 1d4 fire damage. The energy beam has a range increment of 20 feet. On a critical hit, the target takes [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) equal to the number of weapon damage dice. Your eye beam does not add critical specialization effects.

**Enhancement** You channel greater power. Increase the damage die of your energy beam by one step, from 1d4 to 1d6.

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) An automaton character with the Reinforced Chassis feat uses their unarmored proficiency._

</column>

---

Your body is designed to be particularly resilient. Your chassis is medium armor in the [plate](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=4) armor group that grants a +4 item bonus to AC, a Dex cap of +1, a check penalty of –2, a speed penalty of –5 feet, and a Strength value of 16. You can never wear other armor or remove your chassis; however, you still don't become fatigued from sleeping. Finally, you can etch armor runes onto your chassis as normal.

**Enhancement** Your chassis becomes difficult to overcome. You gain the Chassis Deflection reaction.

<document level="2" id="action-3658" />
name: Reinforced Chassis

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Integrated Armament](/Feats.aspx?ID=8599) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Automaton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=398" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 41

</column>

---

Your mechanical body houses a weapon or shield that you can quickly draw and stow, leaving you prepared for combat at all times.

You can use a 1-minute activity, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait, to integrate a single, one-handed weapon or shield into one of your arms. You can draw or stow this item as an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action. Creatures don’t automatically see this integrated item when it’s stowed and must actively [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) in order to find it. They take a –2 circumstance penalty to any checks to do so. While you are wielding the item, it can’t be [Disarmed](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) and you can’t drop or [Release](/Actions.aspx?ID=2300) it; you must Interact to store the weapon and free that hand. A creature determined to retrieve the item can do so, but it requires either 1 minute to remove it or extreme violence to your arm—such as physically removing portions of the limb. You can only have one integrated armament at a time, though you can use the 1-minute activity to replace the item or swap the arm in which it’s stored.

**Enhancement** Your body has more space for integration. You can now either integrate two one-handed weapons, a one-handed weapon and a shield, or a single two-handed weapon which is split across both arms. You can use a single Interact action to draw or store both integrated armaments. When you draw a single weapon, you can choose to hold it with either one hand or both hands.
name: Integrated Armament

markdown:
**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 41 **Prerequisites** [Warrior Automaton](/Heritages.aspx?ID=432) heritage **Requirements** You have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90). --- You channel magical power from your core into your arm, empowering your attack as you attempt to slam your foe into the ground. Attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the foe’s Fortitude DC. You take a –2 circumstance penalty to your check if the target is one size larger than you and a –4 circumstance penalty if it’s larger than that. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your check if the target is one size smaller than you and a +4 circumstance bonus if it’s smaller than that. **Critical Success** You slam the foe down and the magical energy overwhelms it. The creature is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), becomes [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round, and takes damage equal to 2d6 plus your Strength modifier. The foe is no longer [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by you.
**Success** You slam the foe down. The creature is knocked prone and takes damage equal to your Strength modifier. The foe is no longer grabbed or restrained by you.
**Failure** You are unable to slam the creature, but your hold on the creature remains.
**Critical Failure** The creature breaks free and is no longer grabbed or restrained by you. **Enhancement** Your arms better channel your core’s power. You no longer take penalties for attempting to slam larger foes. Your foe takes damage equal to 2d6 plus your Strength modifier on a success (or double that on a critical success).
name: Arcane Slam
requirement_markdown: You have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90).

Throughout the day, your body produces powerful projectiles within your chassis. You fire them all at once in either a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) or a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against the higher of your class DC or Spell DC. Foes in the area take 6d6 piercing damage. This increases to 7d6 at 12th level, 8d6 at 15th level, and 9d6 at 18th level.

**Enhancement** The projectiles regenerate quicker, they are much more powerful, and you can refine the metallic content to harm certain creatures. You can use Rain of Bolts once per hour instead of once per day. The damage increases to 12d6 and each time you use the action, you can choose [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915), [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916), or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920). The damage from Rain of Bolts is treated as the metal you chose. At 20th level, the damage increases to 13d6.

<column gap="tiny">

You’ve attuned your core to allow you to phase in and out of the [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=33), allowing you to teleport periodically. You can cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) once per hour as a 4th-level innate [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) spell.

**Enhancement** Your attunement allows you to teleport in a series of quick jumps rather than in one big leap. You can expend your hourly use of _dimension door_ as a free action when you begin your turn. If you do, your Strides are augmented until the end of your turn, allowing you to instantly teleport to any point you could reach with your Speed instead of traversing normally to the location. While augmented, your Strides gain the [conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33) and [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) traits. Your augmented Strides don’t trigger reactions that can be triggered by move actions or upon leaving or entering a square, unless those reactions trigger on teleportation.
name: Astral Blink

[Core Rejuvenation](/Feats.aspx?ID=8609) <actions string="Free Action" />

<traits>
<trait label="Automaton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=398" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Trigger**
You have the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition and are about to attempt a [recovery check](/Rules.aspx?ID=2326).

</column>

---

Your soul taps into your core’s power to push against the grasp of death and allow you to recover consciousness. You’re restored to 1 Hit Point, lose the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) and [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) conditions, and can act normally on this turn. You gain or increase the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition as normal when losing the dying condition in this way.

**Enhancement** Your soul can draw even more power from your core, granting you additional benefits. When you use Core Rejuvenation, you also gain a number of [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to three times your level. These Hit Points remain for 1 minute.
name: Core Rejuvenation

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Core Cannon](/Feats.aspx?ID=8612) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Automaton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=398" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 43

**Frequency**
once per day

</column>

---

Your body transforms into a powerful magical cannon. While immobile as a cannon, you can fire blasts of energy directly from your core, devastating your foes. You become [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until you use an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to revert back to your standard form. While in your cannon form, the only actions you can use are to Strike with an energy blast unarmed attack or to Interact to revert back to your standard form. You can remain in your cannon form for up to 1 minute or until you take the action to revert back to your standard form, whichever comes first.

Energy blasts are a special ranged unarmed attack. You can only make energy blast Strikes while you’re in your cannon form. Your energy blasts deal 3d8 fire damage and 3d6 force damage, which increases to 4d8 fire damage and 3d6 force damage at level 20. You gain the item bonus to attack rolls with your energy blasts from the highest [_potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) rune on any [_handwraps of mighty blows_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086) you are wearing or any weapon you are wielding, but striking and property runes have no effect on your energy blasts. Energy blasts have a range increment of 120 feet. On a critical hit with an energy blast, the target takes 10 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). Your energy blast doesn’t add critical specialization effects. If it matters for an effect dependent on weapon damage dice, an energy blast’s number of weapon damage dice is three, or four at level 20.
name: Core Cannon

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Overwatch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8620) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 50

**Archetype** [Overwatch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=356)

**Prerequisites**
expert in Perception

</column>

---

You have an innate knack for tactical observation, and you’ve innovated to apply that knack in a variety of ways that allow you to assist your allies. With the help of enhanced visual gear (such as a specially modified telescope, goggled visor, or binoculars), you’re keenly aware of the movement of combatants on the battlefield. You quickly and efficiently disperse that information to your nearby allies so they can use it to direct their own observations and reactions to danger. 

You’re surrounded by an overwatch field aura in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Your overwatch field aura grants you increased perception and allows you to call out warnings to your allies within the aura to point out threats. This aura has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) traits. You and allies within your overwatch field receive a +2 circumstance bonus when using Perception for Initiative checks.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until after you have gained at least two other feats from the overwatch archetype.

<title level="2">Overwatch Dedication leads to...</title>
[Control Tower](/Feats.aspx?ID=8627), [Forewarn](/Feats.aspx?ID=8625), [Master Spotter](/Feats.aspx?ID=8626), [Spyglass Modification](/Feats.aspx?ID=8621), [Swift Intervention](/Feats.aspx?ID=8622), [Topple Giants](/Feats.aspx?ID=8623), [Wide Overwatch](/Feats.aspx?ID=8624)
name: Overwatch Dedication

    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spyglass Modification](/Feats.aspx?ID=8621) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Overwatch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8620)

</column>

---

Your enhanced visual gear includes a regulating scope and reflectors, combined in an ingenuous setup that allows you to see around corners within the area of your overwatch field. This permits your overwatch field to extend around a single corner or turn and allows you to see around that corner as well. This ability doesn’t let you see through walls or other barriers. You can’t use it to see around a second or further corner, and it doesn’t allow you to make ranged attacks or perform other actions that require [line of effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=2382) with your target.
name: Spyglass Modification

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Swift Intervention](/Feats.aspx?ID=8622) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 50

**Archetype** [Overwatch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=356)

**Prerequisites**
[Overwatch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8620)

**Trigger**
An ally within your overwatch field fails in their attempt to [Arrest a Fall](/Actions.aspx?ID=2308), [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369), [Grab a Ledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313), or [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=2371)

**Requirements**
You are wielding a ranged weapon.

</column>

---

You’re burdened with noticing potential threats at all times. Because of this, you’ve learned that a well-placed shot can prevent disaster. You make a shot to either pin an ally’s clothing to stop a fall or secure them against a ledge, or you bounce an attack off their armor to correct their course as they stumble while attempting to [balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) or [fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312). 

Make a single ranged attack roll against the DC of the triggering check; your ally uses the result of your ranged attack roll in place of their original roll. You must have [line of effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=2382) to your ally, and your attack roll includes any normal bonuses or penalties for a ranged attack with that weapon (such as the penalty for making a ranged attack beyond your first range increment).
name: Swift Intervention
requirement_markdown: You are wielding a ranged weapon.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Topple Giants](/Feats.aspx?ID=8623) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Archetype** [Overwatch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=356)

**Prerequisites**
[Overwatch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8620)

</column>

---

When you observe an ally try and fail to execute a strategic maneuver against your enemies, you coordinate with your other allies to finish the job, using the opening from the first attempt to keep your foe off-balance and ensure your eventual success.

When an ally within your overwatch field attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) an opponent within your overwatch field and fails, you coordinate efforts to provide a +2 circumstance bonus to the next [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check rolled by a different ally to attempt the same action on the same target before that target’s next turn.
name: Topple Giants

<traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Forewarn](/Feats.aspx?ID=8625) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 51

**Archetype** [Overwatch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=356)

**Frequency**
once per minute

**Prerequisites**
[Overwatch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8620); master in Perception

**Trigger**
An enemy within your overwatch field attempts an attack against an ally who is also within your overwatch field.

</column>

---

Your foresight and planning are more valuable than armor as you direct your ally away from danger, but there’s a limit to how often you can guide your allies away from a foe’s relentless assaults. The triggering attack roll targets your Perception DC instead of your ally’s AC. Though this allows your ally to avoid taking penalties to their AC, it doesn’t remove any conditions or other effects causing such penalties. For example, an enemy with sneak attack would still deal extra damage to your ally if they are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), even though they wouldn’t take the –2 circumstance penalty when defending against the attack.
name: Forewarn

Your keen senses and ability to process battlefield information have dramatically improved, allowing you to drastically reduce the advantage of unseen foes. You and allies in your overwatch field gain a +2 circumstance bonus when using the Seek action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) creatures within the overwatch field. You and your allies don’t have to succeed at a [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to target a [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) creature within your overwatch field. When you or an ally targets a hidden creature in your overwatch field, reduce the DC of the flat check to 5.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Converge](/Feats.aspx?ID=8628) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 51

**Archetype** [Overwatch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=356)

**Prerequisites**
; master in Perception

**Trigger**
You make a successful ranged attack against an opponent who is within your overwatch field, and who is within reach of one or more of your allies who is also within your overwatch field.

</column>

---

Informing your ally of an opening created by your shot, you coordinate to set them up to hit the target at the same time. Your ally can make a melee Strike against the triggering foe as a reaction. This Strike doesn’t count toward that ally’s multiple attack penalty, and their multiple attack penalty doesn’t apply to this Strike. If your ally’s Strike is successful, combine the damage from your successful ranged attack with the damage from your ally’s melee attack for the purpose of determining resistances and weaknesses.
name: Converge

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sterling Dynamo Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8629) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 52

**Archetype** [Sterling Dynamo](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=357)

</column>

---

You obtain a sterling dynamo prosthetic, which allows you to replace or augment a limb, or even add a simple new non‑prehensile limb, such as a tail. While your sterling dynamo prosthetic can replace a missing prehensile limb, such as an arm, choosing to add a new limb can never increases your character’s number of hands or prehensile limbs beyond two, even in combination with other abilities or effects.

Your sterling dynamo prosthesis is especially optimized for combat, granting you a dynamo melee unarmed attack that is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) weapon group and is made of [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) (which makes it more effective against certain types of creatures, such as devils and werewolves).

You have several options to customize your sterling dynamo. First, choose whether you have a power driver dynamo, which deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage and has the shove trait, or a percussive striker dynamo, which deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage and has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) and [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602) traits.

Most dynamos are automated, so they don’t require a free hand to use, like other unarmed attacks. However, you can instead choose to make your dynamo be manually controlled via a handheld switch, lever, or other device; to make an attack with a manually controlled dynamo, you must have a hand free to operate it, but the benefit is great control and a more devastating strike. This increases the damage die by one size, to a 1d8 for a power drive dynamo or 1d6 for a percussive striker dynamo. Arm dynamos are always manual, but they use the hand on that arm to operate the dynamo when making strikes. Manual control is required only in the heat of combat, not for less strenuous adventuring or everyday activities.

You need to keep your sterling dynamo wound, but the process isn’t particularly onerous, so in most situations, it won’t be an issue. If you spend 1 minute winding up your dynamo, it can remain active for 24 hours before needing additional winding. However, enemies can attempt to Disable the dynamo, which has a level equal to your own, to reduce the remaining operational time by 1 hour, or 2 hours on a critical success. Certain other abilities that adversely affect technology might also reduce the dynamo’s remaining operational time. When your dynamo is out of operational time, you can still use it as a normal prosthetic, but you cannot use your dynamo unarmed attack or any other abilities from this archetype until you wind the dynamo again. You can wind the dynamo for 1 minute at any time to return it to the maximum 24 hours of operational time.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the sterling dynamo archetype.

<title level="2">Sterling Dynamo Dedication leads to...</title>
[Golem Dynamo](/Feats.aspx?ID=8634), [Modular Dynamo](/Feats.aspx?ID=8630), [Piston Punch](/Feats.aspx?ID=8631), [Soaring Dynamo](/Feats.aspx?ID=8635), [Stasian Smash](/Feats.aspx?ID=8632)
name: Sterling Dynamo Dedication

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<additional-info>
**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 52

</additional-info>

---

<summary>
You obtain a sterling dynamo prosthetic, which allows you to replace or augment a limb, or even add a simple new non‑prehensile limb, such as a tail.
</summary>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Modular Dynamo](/Feats.aspx?ID=8630) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 53

**Archetype** [Sterling Dynamo](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=357)

**Prerequisites**
[Sterling Dynamo Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8629)

</column>

---

Your dynamo has modular configurations, allowing you to swap between various different possibilities with ease in order to adapt to various situations in combat. Your dynamo attack gains the [modular](/Traits.aspx?ID=807) trait, as well as one of the following configurations of your choice: power driver (1d6 bludgeoning damage; shove), percussive striker (1d4 bludgeoning damage; [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), rotating sickle (1d6 slashing damage; [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), or entangling barbs (1d6 piercing damage; [grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619)). If you have a manually controlled dynamo, these damage dice increase by 1 size, as usual, and you can also choose the extendable baton (1d4 bludgeoning damage; finesse, [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), which has the damage increase already factored in.

When you use an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to switch configurations using the modular trait, you switch between the initial configuration of dynamo you chose with the Sterling Dynamo Dedication and the new configuration you chose with Modular Dynamo.

**Special** You can select this feat multiple times. Each time, you choose another configuration and add it to the list of options you can choose when you use an [Interact](/Rules.aspx?ID=2151) action with the [modular](/Traits.aspx?ID=807) trait.
name: Modular Dynamo

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

---

You lash out with a powerful punch, extending your dynamo to a greater length in order to attack two creatures in a row. Make a single bludgeoning or piercing dynamo Strike and compare the attack roll result to the ACs of up to two foes. The first foe must be within your melee reach and the second foe must be adjacent to the first foe in a straight line away from you. Roll damage only once and apply it to each creature you hit. A Piston Punch counts as two attacks for your multiple attack penalty. Reduce the operational time of your sterling dynamo by 1 hour.
name: Piston Punch

<traits>

</column>

---

You’ve modified your dynamo with Stasian coils, allowing you to make a powerful attack that crackles with electricity. Make a dynamo Strike. On a success, the Strike deals an additional 1d12 electricity damage to the target, as well as 1d4 electricity damage to up to two other foes within 10 feet as sparks leap across the gaps. If you critically fail your dynamo Strike, you take 1d12 electricity damage. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty. At 18th level, the additional electricity damage to the target increases to 2d12, and the additional electricity damage to the other foes increases to 2d4. Reduce the operational time of your sterling dynamo by 1 hour.
name: Stasian Smash

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

---

You use glimmering gears and gnashing mechanisms to manufacture snares using specialized parts. Your snares include clockwork and steam-powered cogwheels and gears, and their gush of steam can hinder those you ensnare. If you choose to construct a snare using gears and a creature fails their saving throw against the snare, all creatures are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to that creature for 1 round, as a burst of steam obscures its vision.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the [snarecrafter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=265) or trapsmith archetypes.

<title level="2">Trapsmith Dedication leads to...</title>
[Finessed Features](/Feats.aspx?ID=8641), [Gear Gnash](/Feats.aspx?ID=8637), [Propeller Attachment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8638), [Recycled Cogwheel](/Feats.aspx?ID=8639), [Repurposed Parts](/Feats.aspx?ID=8640)
name: Trapsmith Dedication

</column>

---

Snares you create have complicated inner workings that are quite difficult to disable. Creatures attempting to [Disable](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411) your snares that use gears must succeed at an additional [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check in order to disable the snare. On a failed check, while the snare doesn’t fully trigger, the grinding mechanisms deal bludgeoning damage to the creature equal to half your level. The snare still triggers on a critical failure, as normal.
name: Gear Gnash

[Propeller Attachment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8638) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />

**Prerequisites**
[Trapsmith Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8636); Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

You’ve mastered attaching clockwork propellers to your daily quick-deploy snares that use gears. This allows you to place your snares in the air or underwater, where they remain in place for up to 10 minutes, after which the propeller runs out of power and they fall or sink. Unless you use additional precautions to hide it, a propeller snare’s location is usually obvious.
name: Propeller Attachment

    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

**Prerequisites**
[Trapsmith Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8636)

</column>

---

You’ve learned how to build snares that trigger based on visual stimuli. When you construct a snare using gears, you can cause it to trigger based on the visual features of a creature. For example, you could lay a snare that can only be triggered by a Large or Larger creatures, or one that only activates when a creature wearing red clothing would trigger it. You can use this ability to specify your snares to trigger on creatures that typically do not trigger snares, such as creatures smaller than Small, though the creature must still otherwise satisfy the snare’s trigger as normal.
name: Finessed Features

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />

**Prerequisites**

</column>

---

You are equally at home piloting any vehicle, adapting yourself instantly to varied controls, movements, and handling. Whenever piloting a vehicle requires a [Piloting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or Sailing Lore check, you can use your Driving Lore proficiency instead. You can use Dexterity in place of Intelligence when attempting piloting checks with Driving Lore. You become an expert in Driving Lore. At 7th level, you become a master in Driving Lore, and at 15th level, you become legendary in Driving Lore.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the trick driver archetype.

<title level="2">Trick Driver Dedication leads to...</title>
[Drive-By Attack](/Feats.aspx?ID=8648), [Express Driver](/Feats.aspx?ID=8643), [Power Slide](/Feats.aspx?ID=8645), [Push It](/Feats.aspx?ID=8646), [Roadkill](/Feats.aspx?ID=8647), [Take the Wheel](/Feats.aspx?ID=8644)
name: Trick Driver Dedication

</title>

---

You can optimize your vehicle’s performance, coaxing extra speed and choosing paths to avoid slowdowns. When calculating your travel speed for the day while piloting a vehicle, you can attempt a [Driving Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check to increase your vehicle’s travel speed. The DC is determined by the GM but is typically based on the vehicle’s piloting DC or a difficulty based on the environment, whichever is harder. On a success, increase your vehicle’s travel speed by half. This has no effect on your vehicle’s movement in encounters.
name: Express Driver

You are adept at designing and maintaining mechanical vehicles, and you lavish your attention and ingenuity on one vehicle in particular. You become an expert in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37), and you choose one vehicle you own—or your party owns collectively—to be your signature vehicle: the vehicle you spend time customizing to grant it various advantages. You can spend 1 week of downtime with a new vehicle to designate it as your signature vehicle instead. Due to your amazing customizations and adjustments, pilots gain a +1 circumstance bonus to any piloting checks they make to control your signature vehicle. If you are a master in Crafting, this bonus increases to +2.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the mechanic archetype.

<title level="2">Vehicle Mechanic Dedication leads to...</title>
[Efficient Controls](/Feats.aspx?ID=8653), [Engine Bay](/Feats.aspx?ID=8650), [Impervious Vehicle](/Feats.aspx?ID=8654), [Miraculous Flight](/Feats.aspx?ID=8655), [Patch Job](/Feats.aspx?ID=8651), [Superior Propulsion](/Feats.aspx?ID=8652)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Superior Propulsion](/Feats.aspx?ID=8652) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 57

**Archetype** [Vehicle Mechanic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=360)

**Prerequisites**
[Vehicle Mechanic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8649)

</column>

---

By experimenting with the effects of momentum gained from your signature vehicle’s movement, you have learned to get more from propulsion than just speed when the pilot pushes the vehicle to its limit. Each time the pilot [Drives](/Actions.aspx?ID=3517) your signature vehicle as a 2- or 3-action activity and succeeds at the Piloting check, the vehicle produces an additional effect, depending on its method of propulsion. If your signature vehicle has more than one propulsion method, the pilot can select which benefit to gain each time they succeed at the Piloting check to Drive the vehicle as a 2- or 3-action activity.

**Alchemical** The caustic runoff from the alchemical engines is now weaponized. Creatures adjacent to your signature vehicle when it begins its movement take acid damage equal to your level.

**Clockwork** More resilient gears allow the vehicle to remain functional, even when damaged. If your signature vehicle is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), it does not need to make the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to avoid losing operating time.

**Magical** The residual magic from your signature vehicle interferes with [hostile](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) spells. Pilots, crew, and passengers of the vehicle gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against spells until the start of the pilot’s next turn. Pulled You have eased the amount of effort needed for those pulling your signature vehicle to move it. The vehicle gains a +10 circumstance bonus to its Speed.

**Rowed** Reinforced ports protect the rowers below decks from harm. Rowers of your signature vehicle who are below decks have [greater cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) from attacks from outside the vehicle and are not damaged when the vehicle takes collision damage.

**Steam** The outlet of steam forms a cloud around the vehicle as it moves. For the duration of its movement, your signature vehicle is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62).

**Wind** The draft of the wind helps the vehicle turn. During this movement, your signature vehicle can turn normally, rather than needing to move in a straight [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) as normal for a 2- or 3-action Drive.
name: Superior Propulsion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Efficient Controls](/Feats.aspx?ID=8653) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 57

**Archetype** [Vehicle Mechanic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=360)

**Prerequisites**
[Vehicle Mechanic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8649); Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

By reducing redundant systems and streamlining essential functions, you have made it easier for the vehicle to operate with fewer crew. Reduce the number of crew required to operate your signature vehicle by 25%. For example, you would reduce a sailing ship from needing 1 pilot and 8 crew to 1 pilot and 6 crew. This adjustment only affects the number of crew needed to operate the vehicle; it doesn’t reduce the vehicle’s need for a pilot.
name: Efficient Controls

[Impervious Vehicle](/Feats.aspx?ID=8654) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Vehicle Mechanic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8649)

</column>

---

From armor plating and flexible components, to reinforced materials and backup redundant functionality, you have built safeguards to protect your vehicle from the rigors of combat. Your signature vehicle gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and Fortitude saves, and increases its Hit Points by an amount equal to twice your level.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Miraculous Flight](/Feats.aspx?ID=8655) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 57

**Archetype** [Vehicle Mechanic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=360)

**Prerequisites**
[Vehicle Mechanic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8649)

</column>

---

With superior engineering and magical application, you have freed your signature vehicle from the bonds of gravity, allowing it to soar through the sky like a dragon. The vehicle gains magical propulsion and a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) equal to its fastest current Speed. If your signature vehicle already had a fly Speed, reduce the distance the vehicle needs to make a 90-degree turn to half the usual length (typically half the length of the vehicle).
name: Miraculous Flight

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 111 **Requirements** You’re wielding a loaded firearm. --- Sometimes taking the shortest distance between two points involves removing an obstacle or two. You shoot your firearm at a lock within 10 feet. Make an attack roll against the DC required to [Pick the Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412). **Critical Success** You open the lock, or you achieve two successes toward opening a complex lock.
**Success** You open the lock, or you achieve one success toward opening a complex lock.
**Failure** You fail to open the lock, and your shot makes it harder to open. Future attempts to Pick the Lock or Blast the Lock take a –2 circumstance penalty.
**Critical Failure** You fail to open the lock, and your shot makes it much harder to open. Future attempts to Pick the Lock or Blast the Lock take a –4 circumstance penalty.
name: Blast Lock
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded firearm.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cover Fire](/Feats.aspx?ID=8657) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 111

**Archetypes** [Sniping Duo](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=121) (Level 4), [Sniping Duo](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=368) (Level 4)

**Frequency**
once per round

**Requirements**
You’re wielding a loaded firearm or crossbow.

</column>

---

You lay down suppressive fire to protect allies by forcing foes to take cover from your wild attacks. Make a firearm or crossbow Strike; the target must decide before you roll your attack whether it will duck out of the way. If the target ducks, it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against your attack, or a +4 circumstance bonus to AC if it has cover. It also takes a –2 circumstance penalty to ranged attack rolls until the end of its next turn. If the target chooses not to duck, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your attack roll for that Strike.
name: Cover Fire
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded firearm or crossbow.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Munitions Crafter](/Feats.aspx?ID=8661) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 111

</column>

---

Bullets and bombs can be scarce in some parts, so you’ve learned to make your own. You become trained in Crafting and gain the [alchemist’s infused reagents](/Classes.aspx?ID=56) class feature, gaining a number of reagents each day equal to your level. You also gain the [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=5117) feat and four additional formulas for 1st-level alchemical items. 

You gain infused reagents (a pool of reagents usable to make alchemical items) and advanced alchemy (allowing you to make alchemical items during your daily preparations without the normal cost or time expenditure). You gain batches of infused reagents per day equal to your level, which you can use to create only bombs or alchemical ammunition. Your advanced alchemy level for creating these is 1 and doesn’t increase on its own. If you use a batch of infused reagents to create basic level-0 ammunition such as [black powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1190) cartridges or black powder doses, you produce 10 rounds of ammunition.

<title level="2">Munitions Crafter leads to...</title>
[Munitions Machinist](/Feats.aspx?ID=8677)
name: Munitions Crafter

[Sword and Pistol](/Feats.aspx?ID=8662) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 111

**Archetypes** [Pistol Phenom](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=120) (Level 4), [Pistol Phenom](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=367) (Level 4)

</column>

---

You’re comfortable wielding a firearm or crossbow in one hand and a melee weapon in the other, combining melee attacks with shots from the firearm. When you make a successful ranged [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against an enemy within your reach with your one-handed firearm or one-handed crossbow, that enemy is flat-footed against your next melee attack with a one-handed melee weapon.

When you make a successful melee Strike against an enemy with your one-handed melee weapon, the next ranged Strike you make against that enemy with a one-handed firearm or one-handed crossbow doesn’t trigger reactions that would trigger on a ranged attack but not a melee attack, such as [Attack of Opportunity](/Actions.aspx?ID=8). Either of these benefits is lost if not used by the end of your next turn.
name: Sword and Pistol

Your quick gestures and flair for performance distract your opponent, leaving it vulnerable to your follow-up attacks. You [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=1434) against an opponent within the required weapon’s first range increment, rather than an opponent within melee reach. If you succeed, the foe is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against your melee and ranged attacks, rather than only your melee attacks. On a critical failure, you’re flat-footed against the target’s melee and ranged attacks, rather than only its melee attacks.

You’re wielding a loaded firearm.

</column>

---

Who needs words when the roar of a gun is so much more succinct? You attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) a foe by firing your weapon into the air, using the firearm’s maximum range rather than the usual range of 30 feet. This check doesn’t take the –4 circumstance penalty if the target doesn’t share a language with you.

requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded firearm.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Alchemical Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=8669) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 112

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) For the gunslinger’s Alchemical Shot feat and any similar abilities that change a weapon’s damage type, only the primary damage’s type is affected; any secondary effects (such as precision damage or damage applied from a rune) are not affected._

**Requirements**
You have an alchemical bomb worn or in one hand, and are wielding a firearm or crossbow

</column>

---

You’ve practiced a technique for mixing alchemical bombs with your loaded shot. You [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to retrieve the bomb (if it’s not already in your hand) and pour its contents onto your ammunition, consuming the bomb, then resume your grip on the required weapon. The Strike deals damage of the same type as the bomb (for instance, fire damage for alchemist’s fire), and it deals an additional 1d6 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) of the same type as the bomb. If the Strike is a failure, you take 1d6 damage of the same type as the bomb you used, and the firearm misfires.

The persistent damage increases to 2d6 if the bomb is 11th level or higher, or 3d6 if it’s 17th level or higher.
name: Alchemical Shot
requirement_markdown: You have an alchemical bomb worn or in one hand, and are wielding a firearm or crossbow

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Requirements**
You’re wielding two weapons, each of which can be either a loaded one-handed firearm or loaded one‑handed crossbow.

</column>

---

Your shots hit simultaneously. Make two Strikes, one with each of your two ranged weapons, each using your current multiple attack penalty. Both Strikes must have the same target. 

If both attacks hit, combine their damage and then add any applicable effects from both weapons. You add any precision damage, only once, to the attack of your choice. Combine the damage from both Strikes and apply resistances and weaknesses only once. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty.
name: Paired Shots
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding two weapons, each of which can be either a loaded one-handed firearm or loaded one‑handed crossbow.

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 114 **Prerequisites** [way of the pistolero](/Ways.aspx?ID=8) --- With a stern call, carefully chosen barb, or some other challenging declaration, you demand your foe’s attention in a duel. Choose an opponent within 30 feet who you can perceive and can hear you, and make your choice of a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check against the target’s Will DC. No matter the result of the check, the foe is temporarily immune to your Pistolero’s Challenge until noon the next day. **Success** Both you and the opponent gain a +2 status bonus to damage rolls with Strikes made against each other. If you’re a master in the skill you used for the check, the status bonus to damage rolls increases to +3, and if you’re legendary, it increases to +4. You can have only one challenge in effect at a time; challenging a new opponent ends this effect on any current target. Otherwise, the effect lasts until one of you is defeated, flees, or the encounter ends.
**Critical Failure** You become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 and can’t use this ability again for 1 minute. Pistolero's Challenge leads to... [Come at Me!](/Feats.aspx?ID=8703)
name: Pistolero's Challenge

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 114 **Requirements** You’re wielding a loaded firearm that has the [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) trait. --- You pack your weapon with additional shot and powder, creating a risky but devastating wave of destruction. Make a ranged [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the firearm. The firearm’s range increment increases by 20 feet and the radius of its scatter increases by 20 feet. The Strike gains the following failure conditions. **Failure** The firearm misfires, but it doesn’t cause the other critical failure effects listed below.
**Critical Failure** The firearm misfires and also explodes. It becomes [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), and it deals its normal weapon damage to all creatures in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) centered on the firearm, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against your class DC. This damage includes any from the weapon’s [fundamental](/Rules.aspx?ID=3167) and [property](/Rules.aspx?ID=3168) runes.
name: Scatter Blast
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded firearm that has the [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) trait.

---

You make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with your firearm and create a cloud of smoke in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) centered on your location. You slice or smash your opponent with the melee portion of your weapon before pulling the trigger at point-blank range. Make a melee Strike with the required weapon. If the [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) is successful, you can immediately make a ranged Strike against the same target with a +2 circumstance bonus to the attack roll. This counts as two attacks toward your multiple attack penalty, but you don’t apply the multiple attack penalty until after making both attacks.

markdown:
**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 115 --- You target a specific area of your foe’s anatomy to debilitating effect. Declare a part of your foe’s anatomy to target and make a ranged weapon [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167). If you hit and deal damage, the target takes one of the following effects, based on the body part you targeted. The GM should use the stated effects as a guideline when attacking creatures with stranger anatomy—for instance, applying the “arms” effect to a creature’s tentacles if those are what the creature uses to attack.
  • **Arms** The target is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 until the end of your next turn. On a critical hit, it’s also enfeebled 1 for 1 minute.
  • **Head** The target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 until the end of your next turn. On a critical hit, it’s also stupefied 1 for 1 minute.
  • **Legs** The target takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the end of your next turn. On a critical hit, it also takes a –5-foot penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute.
  • **Wings** If the target is [flying](/Rules.aspx?ID=3374) using its wings, it immediately falls 20 feet, or 40 feet on a critical hit. The fall is gradual enough that if the target hits the ground, it takes no damage from the fall.
name: Called Shot

**Trigger**
An ally within the first range increment of your firearm or crossbow is hit by an attack, and you can see the attacker.

**Requirements**
You’re wielding a loaded firearm or crossbow.

</column>

---

You’re always ready to take a quick shot to deflect a weapon or distract a foe enough to render them off target. The ally gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their AC against the triggering attack. You use this reaction after the result of the attack roll is revealed, so you know whether the +2 will make a sufficient difference.
name: Deflecting Shot
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded firearm or crossbow.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rebounding Assault](/Feats.aspx?ID=8691) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 115

**Requirements**
You’re wielding a loaded firearm or loaded crossbow in one hand and a bludgeoning or slashing melee weapon in the other.

</column>

---

You hurl your melee weapon at an opponent, then fire a bullet into the weapon’s hilt, making it bounce back to your grasp. Make a thrown ranged [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the melee weapon, then a ranged Strike with your firearm. Both Strikes use your current multiple attack penalty and this counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty. If the melee weapon doesn’t already have the thrown trait, it gains the thrown 10 feet trait during a Rebounding Assault. 

If both attacks are successful, the bolt or bullet hits the thrown melee weapon instead of your target, adding its force into one attack. Combine the damage from both Strikes, using the thrown weapon’s damage type, and add an additional 1d6 precision damage. The impact sends the melee weapon rebounding off the target and back to your hand. 

In any circumstance other than both attacks hitting, carry out the individual Strikes normally. The thrown weapon doesn’t rebound and remains in the target’s space as normal for thrown weapons.
name: Rebounding Assault
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded firearm or loaded crossbow in one hand and a bludgeoning or slashing melee weapon in the other.

**Trigger**
An ally misses with a ranged attack that uses a thrown weapon or ammunition. The ally must be willing to accept your assistance, you must see the attack’s target, and the attack’s target must be within the first range increment of your firearm or crossbow.

</column>

---

Seeing your ally’s attack about to go astray, you fire your weapon to right its course. Discharge your firearm and roll a d20. Your ally uses this roll instead of their own, and the attack ignores bonuses the target would gain against the attack from [lesser or standard cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372).
name: Redirecting Shot

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 116 **Archetypes** [Bullet Dancer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=117) (Level 12), [Pistol Phenom](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=120) (Level 12), [Unexpected Sharpshooter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=123) (Level 10), [Bullet Dancer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=364) (Level 12), [Pistol Phenom](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=367) (Level 12), [Unexpected Sharpshooter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=370) (Level 10) **Requirements** You’re wielding a loaded crossbow or firearm. --- You shoot not at your foe but at an object nearby. Make a Strike with your firearm against an AC equal to an easy [DC for your level](/Rules.aspx?ID=681). On a success, instead of hitting your foe, your attack hits an object behind or to the side of your target, creating one of the following effects. The GM might determine that a different AC is appropriate or that a particular effect isn’t a valid option, such as using the explosive barrel option when there’s no such object on the battlefield. The GM should inform you if a Trick Shot is feasible before you spend your actions, since your trained eye can easily recognize loose or volatile objects.
  • **Dislodge Object** Your attack knocks an unattended object of no more than 2 Bulk out of position, moving it up to 10 feet in a direction of your choice. For example, the weapon could knock a wizard’s crystal ball off a table.
  • **Explosive Barrel** Your attack strikes a barrel of expensive rum, a vial of volatile alchemical fluids, a demonic pustule erupting from the earth, or some other explosive object. The object explodes in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385), and creatures in the area take 6d6 damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against your class DC. The damage type is chosen by the GM, based on the exploding object. Increase the damage by 1d6 for every 2 levels you have above 10th.
Trick Shot leads to... [Ranged Disarm](/Feats.aspx?ID=8744)
name: Trick Shot
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded crossbow or firearm.

You specialize in blending into a particular type of environment, able to disappear after taking your shot. Choose either natural or urban terrain. In the chosen terrain, you can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) even without [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) or being [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blast Tackle](/Feats.aspx?ID=8702) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 117

**Prerequisites**
[way of the vanguard](/Ways.aspx?ID=10)

**Requirements**
You’re wielding a two-handed crossbow or a firearm that has the [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=847) or [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) trait.

</column>

---

You throw yourself at an enemy, bracing your weapon against it and pulling it close before releasing a destructive shot. Attempt to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376); if you’re wielding your weapon in both hands, you [Release](/Actions.aspx?ID=2300) one hand to do so. If your Grapple succeeds, you can immediately [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) the target of the Grapple with the required weapon, even if it’s a two-handed weapon you’re holding in one hand. This Strike deals an additional 3d6 precision damage.

As long the creature remains [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), you can use just one hand to [reload](/Rules.aspx?ID=2196) the weapon and Strike that creature with it.
name: Blast Tackle
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a two-handed crossbow or a firearm that has the [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=847) or [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) trait.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dance of Thunder](/Feats.aspx?ID=8704) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 117

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) For the gunslinger’s Dance of Thunder feat, the first set of three actions must be resolved before subsequent sets of actions can be taken._

**Frequency**
once per minute

**Requirements**
You’re wielding a loaded firearm or crossbow and are not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73).

</column>

---

Your steps echo with the thunderous retort of exploding black powder as you dance a dance of death. Take any of the following actions you choose in any order: [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against a target within your firearm’s first range increment, and [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to reload. If you attempted a Strike and it succeeded, you can repeat these three actions again in any order. If you attempted a Strike in the second set and succeeded, you can repeat the actions one last time.

After using Dance of Thunder, you become fatigued for 1 minute.
name: Dance of Thunder
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded firearm or crossbow and are not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73).

</column>

---

You attempt to finish your target with a final, well-placed shot. Make a crossbow or firearm [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167). If you’ve damaged the target within the last minute with the same weapon and you get a critical success on your attack roll, the target must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or die; this is a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) and [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) effect. A creature that survives is temporarily immune for 1 day. Creatures with no head (such as [dullahans](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2964) or [oozes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=453)) are immune to this ability, and creatures with redundant heads (such as [ettins](/Monsters.aspx?ID=204)) might survive the destruction of a head.
name: Headshot

You roll one [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check and compare it against each target’s Perception DC.

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 118

**Prerequisites**
initial deed that allows you to Interact to draw a weapon

</column>

---

No one can react faster than you can pull your trigger. When using your initial deed, instead of [Interacting](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to draw a weapon, you can [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with a firearm or crossbow you’re already wielding. If this Strike hits, the target is also [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of your first turn of the encounter.

**Special** If your initial deed allows you to [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to draw a weapon more than once, this feat allows you to replace one of these Interact actions and use the other one to draw a weapon. You can’t replace both Interact actions with Strikes, but you can draw a loaded firearm or crossbow with one of the actions and immediately shoot it with the other.
name: Hair Trigger
prerequisite_markdown: initial deed that allows you to Interact to draw a weapon

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

An enemy misses you with a ranged attack using a type of ammunition appropriate to your weapon (a bullet if you’re wielding a firearm).

**Requirements**
You’re wielding an unloaded firearm or crossbow.

</column>

---

With a single fluid gesture, you catch a projectile out of the air, load it in your weapon, and shoot it back at the attacker. Interact to load the projectile into your weapon, then make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the required weapon against the triggering opponent. Since you’re using the foe’s ammunition, this Strike applies any special effects that ammunition would have (for instance, if it was [_explosive ammunition_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2923), it would explode in a burst of fire if your Strike hits).
name: Instant Return
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding an unloaded firearm or crossbow.

Your senses are finely tuned to the slightest variations in the air, so much that you can place your shot exactly where you intend to aim it from any range possible with your weapon. When attacking with a ranged weapon in which you have master or greater proficiency, ignore the penalty from its range increment. (The weapon’s maximum range is unchanged.) In addition, if your target has [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) against your attack with such a weapon, it doesn’t gain the bonus to AC if it has lesser cover, gets only the bonus of lesser cover if it has standard cover, or gains only the bonus of standard cover if it has greater cover.

[Reach for the Stars](/Feats.aspx?ID=8716) <actions string="" />

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Black Powder Boost](/Feats.aspx?ID=8670)

</column>

---

The sky’s the limit, as long as you’ve got enough black powder. When you use Black Powder Boost, you can expend additional black powder or ammunition to boost yourself farther. For each dose of black powder or piece of firearm ammunition you spend in addition to your shot, you add another 10 feet to the height or distance of your [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), to a maximum of 5 boosts (a +50-foot status bonus). You must be wearing a dose of black powder or piece of ammunition, or have it in hand, to detonate it for a boost.

You can choose at which points in your Leap you detonate each boost, allowing you to change direction each time. You must then move in a straight [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) until you boost again or end your Leap.
name: Reach for the Stars

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Gunslinger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=406" />
</traits>

[Ricochet Master](/Feats.aspx?ID=8712)

</column>

---

The more obstacles between you and your target, the deadlier your shots become, as you ricochet off multiple surfaces to hit them. When using Ricochet Shot, your shot can bounce off of any surface within your weapon’s first three range increments and the target can’t benefit from cover unless they have total [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) on all sides. 

In addition, a creature must attempt a Will save against your class DC the first time in an encounter you attack it with a Ricochet Shot. If it fails, it’s [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2 and is unable to determine where your shot originated.
name: Ricochet Legend

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Artillerist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8727) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 128

**Archetype** [Artillerst](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=362)

**Prerequisites**
trained in martial weapons

</column>

---

Artillery is a team sport, where every member of the crew has to rely on the other members. At best, a mistake might just waste time. At worst, a misaligned fuse or a badly set pin could cause the whole thing to explode. You’ve taken these lessons to heart, and so your presence on an artillery team assists the entire team at every aspect of the siege weapon’s deployment and usage. If you’re serving on a [siege](/Rules.aspx?ID=1622) weapon crew, you and all other members gain a +2 circumstance bonus to any checks to Load, Aim, move, or Repair the weapon. When you Aim a siege weapon, you can move the weapon’s aim twice as far as normal.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you’ve gained at least two other feats from the artillerist archetype.
name: Artillerist Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Named Artillery](/Feats.aspx?ID=8728) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 128

**Archetype** [Artillerst](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=362)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

It is a tradition among artillerists to name the siege weapon most important to them and closest to their heart—much as a sailor on a ship, the weapon is their livelihood. You’ve gone a step further, and you always make sure that your named artillery has the best possible maintenance and upkeep. You can spend a full day adjusting and working on a single siege weapon to designate it as your named artillery. The siege weapon you designated as your named artillery gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC, Fortitude saves, and Reflex saves as well as additional Hit Points equal to twice your level.

During your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), you must spend at least one hour on maintenance to service your named artillery. If you fail to do so, or if you spend a full day designating a new piece of named artillery, the previous named artillery loses any benefits from this feat. Only one artillerist can designate a particular siege weapon as their named artillery, even if several artillerists are serving on the same siege weapon’s crew (though it’s typically more efficient for a group with several artillerists to divide them up, assigning one to each siege weapon).
name: Named Artillery

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Shorthanded](/Feats.aspx?ID=8729) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 129

**Archetype** [Artillerst](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=362)

</column>

---

Typically, a siege weapon is meant to be run by a crew with a very specific minimum number of members designed to ensure that your crew can account for every possible variable and necessity in loading, aiming, and firing the weapon. In a pinch, however, you can operate it with fewer people, provided you know what you’re doing. You can operate a siege weapon with fewer than the minimum number of crew, at an increasing penalty. You and your crew take a –2 penalty to checks to Load, Aim, Launch, move, or Repair the weapon for each person below the minimum crew. The maximum number of missing minimum crew members you can handle with this feat is 5. For example, if a siege weapon had a minimum crew size of 8, you would still need a crew of 3, and you would take a –10 penalty when doing so.
name: Shorthanded

If you personally contribute an additional Load action to the siege weapon’s loading, above the minimum, you can change a burst into a line twice as long as the size of the [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385), or vice versa (so a 10-foot burst turns into a 20-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), or a 20-foot line turns into a 10-foot burst).

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

While castles are generally obliging enough to stay in one place, out in the field positioning is of paramount importance—a gun in the wrong place is worse than useless. You can [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) with an adjacent mounted siege weapon to increase its Speed by 10 feet for one round.
name: Field Artillery
