markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Live Ammunition](/Feats.aspx?ID=8732) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 129

**Archetype** [Artillerst](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=362)

</column>

---

There are generally few things as ill-advised as being shot out of a cannon or launched by a trebuchet, but people use the tactic every so often in a desperate situation. While it’s more humane to use this strategy with mindless constructs or undead, occasionally an exceedingly foolhardy adventurer demands the opportunity to try it. Loading a creature requires two additional Load actions, which you must conduct personally. The creature must be willing, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) throughout the process, and the siege weapon must be physically capable of firing the creature in question, based on their size and shape. Typically, that means ballistas don’t qualify, for example—nor do auto-catapults which require specifically-sized balls—but normal catapults and onagers work just fine. When you Launch the weapon, if the weapon usually targets an area, you target a single 5-foot square instead. The siege weapon deals its normal damage to its target, or to the modified area, and to the creature fired.
name: Live Ammunition

What you don’t know about artillery isn’t worth knowing and your mastery allows you to perform an impossible number of actions as part of a siege weapon’s crew. You’re permanently quickened. You can only use the extra action to Aim or Launch a siege weapon.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Beast Gunner Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8734) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 130

**Archetype** [Beast Gunner](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=363)

**Prerequisites**
expert with at least one type of firearm; Trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2); you own a beast gun and have slain the type of creature associated with your beast gun in a fair hunt (see archetype page)

</column>

---

You’ve bonded to your beast gun and unlocked the first hints of its hidden potential. You treat all beast guns as martial firearms when determining your proficiency with them, even beast guns that are normally advanced weapons. You can change your bonded beast gun to another beast gun you own each day during your daily preparations, as long as you’ve previously performed a ritual hunt associated with the new beast gun. The beast gun also acts as a conduit, drawing out and amplifying any latent or active magic power you have. You learn to cast spontaneous spells and gain the Cast a Spell activity. You gain a spell repertoire with one cantrip of your choice, from either the arcane or primal spell list. You choose this cantrip from the common spells on your chosen spell list or from other spells you have access to on the list. This cantrip must require a spell attack roll. You’re trained in spell attack rolls and spell DCs for [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) or [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) spells, whichever of the two traditions you chose. Your key spellcasting ability for these spells is Charisma.

If you already cast arcane or primal spells from spell slots, you learn one additional cantrip from that tradition. Your key spellcasting ability for these spells is either Charisma or Intelligence, chosen when you take this feat.

You also gain Spellsling.

**Spellsling** <actions string="Three Actions" /> **Requirements** You’re wielding your bonded beast gun; **Effect** You Cast a Spell that takes 1 or 2 actions to cast and requires a spell attack roll. The effects of the spell do not occur immediately but are imbued into the beast gun you’re wielding. Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with that beast gun. Your spell flies with the ammunition, using your attack roll result to determine the effects of both the Strike and the spell. This counts as two attacks for the purposes of determining your multiple attack penalty, but you don’t apply the penalty until after you’ve completed resolving the attack and spell.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you’ve gained two other feats from the beast gunner archetype.

<title level="2">Beast Gunner Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Beast Gunner Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=8735), [Controlled Bullet](/Feats.aspx?ID=8738), [Drain Vitality](/Feats.aspx?ID=8736)
name: Beast Gunner Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: expert with at least one type of firearm; Trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2); you own a beast gun and have slain the type of creature associated with your beast gun in a fair hunt (see archetype page)

</title>

<traits>

You draw out the bestial magic energy within your bonded beast gun to fortify yourself, purge ongoing harm, and bolster your life force with a sheath of swirling life essence. You gain [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to your level and attempt a [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) against any ongoing persistent damage, using the DC appropriate for particularly effective assistance. Using this ability depletes the magic within your bonded beast gun, preventing you from using any of its activated abilities until the end of your next turn.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Controlled Bullet](/Feats.aspx?ID=8738) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 131

**Archetype** [Beast Gunner](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=363)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Beast Gunner Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8734)

</column>

---

You create a direct magical connection with your beast gun, allowing you to guide the beast gun’s next shot by taking control of the projectile directly, steering it through the air, and swerving and veering it to continue to hit target after target. Make a beast gun [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against a creature within the first range increment. On a hit, the shot tears through the target and continues toward another target you can see. You make a Strike against this new target. The new target must be no farther from the previous target than a distance equal to the beast gun’s range increment. For example, the new target must be within 150 feet of the previous target when attacking with a [drake rifle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1166). On a hit, you can direct the bullet toward a new target.

You can continue to make Strikes against new targets in this same way until your attack misses, at which point your shot dissipates. You can’t make a Strike against a target you already attacked during this use of Controlled Bullet, but you can otherwise continue to make attacks against valid targets until you miss. Each attack counts toward your multiple attack penalty, but don’t increase your penalty until you’ve made all your attacks.
name: Controlled Bullet

[Bullet Dancer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8740) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 132

**Archetype** [Bullet Dancer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=364)

**Prerequisites**
expert in unarmored defense and trained in simple weapons

</column>

---

You’ve learned how to synchronize traditional martial arts and guns. You gain the Bullet Dancer Stance action and are trained in bayonets and reinforced stocks. Whenever your proficiency in any weapon increases to expert or beyond, you gain that new proficiency with bayonets, reinforced stocks, simple firearms and unarmed attacks.

**Bullet Dancer Stance** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=701)) **Requirements** You’re unarmored and wielding a simple firearm, bayonet, or reinforced stock; **Effect** You enter a specialized stance for a unique martial art centered around the use of simple firearms. While in this stance, the only Strikes you can make are those using bayonets, reinforced stocks, and simple firearms. You can use [Flurry of Blows](/Actions.aspx?ID=2817) with these weapons. You can use your other monk feats or monk abilities that normally require unarmed attacks with [bayonets](/Weapons.aspx?ID=186) and [reinforced stocks](/Weapons.aspx?ID=187), so long as the feat or ability doesn’t require a single, specific Strike. You can also use them with simple firearms when attacking within half the first range increment.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you’ve gained two other feats from the bullet dancer dedication archetype.

<title level="2">Bullet Dancer Dedication leads to...</title>
[Bullet Dancer Burn](/Feats.aspx?ID=8741), [Ranged Disarm](/Feats.aspx?ID=8744)
name: Bullet Dancer Dedication

    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bullet Dancer Burn](/Feats.aspx?ID=8741) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 132

**Archetype** [Bullet Dancer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=364)

**Prerequisites**
[Bullet Dancer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8740)

**Requirements**
You’re wielding a firearm with an attached bayonet or reinforced stock.

</column>

---

The fiery blaze of your weapons combined with your unorthodox technique leaves your opponents with little opportunity to capitalize on any gaps in your defenses, or properly defend themselves against your onslaught. While in Bullet Dancer Stance, when you successfully Strike an opponent with a bayonet or reinforced stock attached to your firearm, the next ranged Strike you make against that opponent doesn’t trigger reactions that would trigger on a ranged attack, such as Attack of Opportunity. While in Bullet Dancer Stance, when you make a successful ranged [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against an opponent within your melee reach using a firearm, the next [reinforced stock](/Weapons.aspx?ID=187) or [bayonet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=186) attack attached to that firearm deals an additional 1 [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage and 1 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) per weapon damage die.

The benefit on your next Strike from either use of Bullet Dancer Burn is lost if not used by the end of your next turn.

<title level="2">Bullet Dancer Burn leads to...</title>
[Bullet Dancer Reload](/Feats.aspx?ID=8743)
name: Bullet Dancer Burn
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a firearm with an attached bayonet or reinforced stock.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Demolitionist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8745) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 133

**Archetype** [Demolitionist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=365)

</column>

---

You know how to set explosives to cause the maximum possible damage to buildings and other structures. You become trained in [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or become an expert in it if you were already trained. You gain the Set Explosives action. 

**Set Explosives** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Requirements** You have 1 or 2 bombs in hand; **Effect** You attach the bombs to an inanimate object within your reach, such as a door, wall, or column, and rig them to detonate at a set time. The bombs explode at a specific time you determine (such as after your next action or at the start of your next turn, to a maximum of 1 minute), dealing their damage and splash damage to the inanimate object. Combine this damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses, and this damage ignores an amount of the object’s [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) equal to your level. Any creatures adjacent to the hazard take the bombs’ splash damage, also combined for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. As a reminder, since you didn’t throw the bombs, Calculated Splash and similar effects don’t apply. Making sure your timing is correct requires concentration, so you can’t Set Explosives again while you’re waiting for a previously set bomb to detonate.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you’ve gained two other feats from the demolitionist archetype.

<title level="2">Demolitionist Dedication leads to...</title>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Safety Measures](/Feats.aspx?ID=8746) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 133

**Archetype** [Demolitionist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=365)

**Prerequisites**
[Demolitionist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8745)

</column>

---

You’re often careful to be sure you’re not hoisted on your own petard, or anyone else’s, for that matter. When you have cover from a bomb, you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to damage caused by that bomb equal to half your level, plus a value equal to the circumstance bonus to AC granted by the [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372). You gain these benefits against your own bombs regardless of whether you have cover.
name: Safety Measures

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Explosive Entry](/Feats.aspx?ID=8747) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 133

**Archetype** [Demolitionist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=365)

**Prerequisites**
[Demolitionist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8745); Master in [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

You know exactly how to make a memorable entrance... by detonating a bomb to produce a devastating explosion, of course! You Set Explosives on a door, window, container, or heavy gate. When the explosives detonate, you can attempt an [Engineering Lore](/Traits.aspx?ID=695) check to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) the target. If a bomb would add an item bonus to attack rolls, add that item bonus to your Engineering Lore check. Since you’re blasting your way in, you can’t avoid breaking the object or structure. If you roll a critical success, you get a success instead.
name: Explosive Entry

[Controlled Blast](/Feats.aspx?ID=8748) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Demolitionist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8745); [Calculated Splash](/Feats.aspx?ID=99), [Directional Bombs](/Feats.aspx?ID=104), or [Expanded Splash](/Feats.aspx?ID=109)

</column>

---

You maximize the effectiveness of your explosives by controlling every possible parameter of the blast meticulously. If you have the [Directional Bombs](/Feats.aspx?ID=5779) feats, you can use it with Set Explosives or Demolition Charge, even though you didn’t throw the bombs. If you have the Calculated Splash or Expanded Splash feats, you can apply the additional splash damage to one of the bombs when you Set Explosives or use Demolition Charge, and you can apply the increased splash area from Expanded Splash to all the bombs.
name: Controlled Blast

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Collapse Wall](/Feats.aspx?ID=8749) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 133

**Archetype** [Demolitionist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=365)

**Prerequisites**
[Demolitionist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8745)

**Trigger**
A creature moves into the bombs’ splash area.

**Requirements**
You’re within 30 feet of an area where you rigged bombs on a wall, cliff face, or similar vertical surface with Set Explosives or Demolition Charge.

</column>

---

You detonate the required bombs in order to bring a wall down on a creature. If your bombs deal enough damage to reduce the wall’s Hit Points below its [Broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60) Threshold, the wall partially collapses on the creature. The creature takes bludgeoning damage equal to the damage dealt to the wall ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save; the DC for this save is equal to your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher). On a failure, they must spend an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to dig themselves out of the collapse, and on a critical failure, they must spend 2 Interact actions to do so.
name: Collapse Wall
requirement_markdown: You’re within 30 feet of an area where you rigged bombs on a wall, cliff face, or similar vertical surface with Set Explosives or Demolition Charge.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Firework Technician Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8750) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 134

**Archetype** [Firework Technican](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=366)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

You’ve learned the secrets of making fire and sound bloom using black powder, metals, and paper. You most likely came by this knowledge through formal training with a fireworks house in Tian Xia or a fireworks company from Vudra, though perhaps you mastered fireworks completely by yourself after finding a secret stash in an old shipwreck.

You become trained in [Fireworks Lore](/Traits.aspx?ID=695) or become an expert if you were already trained in it. You gain the [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=5117) feat, a pool of infused reagents equal to your level, and advanced alchemy (allowing you to make alchemical items during your daily preparations without the normal cost or time expenditure). You can use your infused reagents only to make fireworks, Launch a Fireworks Display, and use other feats from this archetype (see below). Your advanced alchemy level is 1. The rules for these are in the [Alchemical Crafting](/Rules.aspx?ID=2634) section on page 258 of the Core Rulebook, and rules for infused reagents and [advanced alchemy are here](/Feats.aspx?ID=6185). If you gain infused reagents from more than one source, you use the highest number of reagents to determine your pool rather than adding them together.

You can also create special fireworks displays using your infused reagents, designed to create distinctive effects you can use to make onlookers marvel and even to gain an advantage in combat. You gain the Launch Fireworks Display action and learn some basic displays, and you can learn additional, more complex displays through other firework technician feats. The DC for any display is equal to your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher.

**Launch Fireworks Display** <actions string="Single Action" /> to <actions string="Three Actions" /> (see below); ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)); Cost 1 or more batches of infused reagents; **Requirements** You have a free hand; **Effect** You set off a fireworks display. You can launch a normal firework to simply create a visual or audible signal within 20 feet or you can choose one of the following special effects. A firework display has the listed traits, and if it costs more than 1 batch of infused reagents, it is noted in its Cost entry.

**Comet** <actions string="Single Action" /> (visual) You shoot a streak of shining light in a 60-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388). All spaces in that line are lit with bright light until the start of your next turn. As part of this action, you can also [Point Out](/Actions.aspx?ID=2315) a single creature in the line, and your allies do not need to hear or understand you.

**Flower** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) You ignite a ring of sparks, creating a shape that might be reminiscent of a flower blooming in the sky. Each enemy with 20 feet of you must attempt a Fortitude save. On a failure, the enemy is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round, and on a critical failure they are dazzled for 2 rounds.

**Salute** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)) You create a startling bang or whistle, adding an auditory component to your fireworks display. When you launch a salute display, you can choose a [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) or [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) ally within 60 feet. If the ally you chose is fascinated, the noise is so violent that it acts as a hostile effect for the purpose of their fascinated condition (though it has no negative repercussions) automatically ending most applications of the fascinated condition. If they’re confused, the sound is so loud and violent that it might snap the ally out of confusion. They can immediately attempt the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to remove the confused condition which normally occurs when a creature is damaged. If the confused condition has special rules that remove the flat check when taking damage or make the flat check harder, those rules also apply to the salute.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you’ve gained two other feats from the fireworks technician archetype.

<title level="2">Firework Technician Dedication leads to...</title>
[Coughing Dragon Display](/Feats.aspx?ID=8751), [Expert Fireworks Crafter](/Feats.aspx?ID=8752), [Goblin Jubilee Display](/Feats.aspx?ID=8754), [Jumping Jenny Display](/Feats.aspx?ID=8753)
name: Firework Technician Dedication

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Coughing Dragon Display](/Feats.aspx?ID=8751) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 135

**Archetype** [Firework Technican](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=366)

**Prerequisites**
[Firework Technician Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8750)

</column>

---

This display emits a cascade of loud bangs or glittering trails. While you initially designed the display to captivate your audience’s attention, it turns out to have a surprisingly effective use in combat, suppressing [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effects behind your magnificent display. You gain the Coughing Dragon fireworks display.

**Coughing Dragon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> **Cost** 2 batches of infused reagents; **Effect** Choose either auditory or visual effects. The display gains that trait, and you attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) one or more effects within 60 feet that have this trait. On a success, the effect is suppressed until the start of your next turn, rather than ending entirely. Use your [Fireworks Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) modifier as your counteract modifier, and your counteract level is equal to half your advanced alchemy level (rounded up). A coughing dragon costs 2 batches of infused reagents rather than 1, but you can increase the cost to 3 batches and spend an additional action to create an even bigger coughing dragon display that attempts to counteract both auditory and visual effects at the same time.
name: Coughing Dragon Display

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Jumping Jenny Display](/Feats.aspx?ID=8753) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 135

[Firework Technician Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8750)

</column>

---

This multi-sequence rocket blasts confetti at a series of altitudes. While the design is intended to delight children of all ages, you’ve found it has an unexpected side effect of confounding enemy fliers. You gain the Jumping Jenny fireworks display.

**Jumping Jenny** <actions string="Single Action" /> **Cost** 2 batches of infused reagents; **Effect** Target a flying creature within 60 feet. Until the start of your next turn, each time that creature attempts to Fly, they must succeed at an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check to [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=2371) against the DC of your fireworks display, or the [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) action is disrupted. If all the creature’s attempts to Fly are disrupted, at the end of its turn, it falls harmlessly to the ground below. A jumping jenny costs 2 batches of infused reagents, rather than 1.
name: Jumping Jenny Display

[Pistol Phenom Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8756) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />

trained in at least one type of one-handed firearm; Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); Trained in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12)

</column>

---

You catch an opponent off-guard by spinning your weapon. You gain the [Pistol Twirl](/Feats.aspx?ID=3162) gunslinger feat, enabling you to Feint against creatures within your weapon’s first range increment. This otherwise serves as Pistol Twirl for the purpose of meeting prerequisites, although as normal, it doesn’t count as another pistol phenom feat for the purpose of meeting Pistol Phenom Dedication’s special entry and taking another archetype. Whenever you [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=1434) with a one-handed firearm, you can choose to attempt a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check instead of a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you’ve gained at least two other feats from the pistol phenom archetype.

<title level="2">Pistol Phenom Dedication leads to...</title>
[Dazzling Bullet](/Feats.aspx?ID=8758), [Gunpowder Gauntlet](/Feats.aspx?ID=8757), [Hot Foot](/Feats.aspx?ID=8759), [Phenom's Verve](/Feats.aspx?ID=8760), [Reach For the Sky](/Feats.aspx?ID=8761), [Whirling Knockdown](/Feats.aspx?ID=8762)
name: Pistol Phenom Dedication

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dazzling Bullet](/Feats.aspx?ID=8758) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 137

**Archetype** [Pistol Phenom](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=367)

**Prerequisites**
[Pistol Phenom Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8756)

</column>

---

Your tricks discombobulate your opponent, leaving spots in their vision from the bright flash of your gunshot reflected straight into their eyes (or other visual organs). Attempt a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check against the target creature’s Will DC and make a ranged firearm Strike against that target. If the Strike deals damage to the target creature and you succeed at the Performance check, the creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
name: Dazzling Bullet

markdown:
**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 137 **Archetype** [Pistol Phenom](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=367) **Prerequisites** [Pistol Phenom Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8756) **Requirements** You’re wielding a loaded firearm. --- Using a carefully aimed shot, you make a creature “dance.” You shoot at the ground near a target’s feet, causing them to react involuntarily; even mindless creatures have unconscious responses to dodge an attack, and you can capitalize off those uncontrollable reflexes to achieve your aim. As they dance to the sweet tune of your pistol’s retort, you ensure your foe can’t use whatever nasty surprise they had planned in store for you and your allies. Make an attack roll against the Reflex DC of a target creature within your firearm’s first range increment. **Critical Success** The creature can’t use reactions, is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to Reflex saves. These effects last until the start of its next turn.
**Success** The creature can’t use reactions until the start of its next turn.
name: Hot Foot
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded firearm.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Phenom's Verve](/Feats.aspx?ID=8760) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Pistol Phenom Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8756)

</column>

---

You’re possessed with an incredibly vivacious spark that separates you not only from ordinary gun users and gunslingers, but even from other phenoms. Using that distinctive verve, when you perform impeccably and control the scene with your abilities, you also manage to set up your foes for disaster and ensure they remain in your sights for any follow-up attacks you want to make against them. If you critically succeed at a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check for Pistol Twirl or a pistol phenom archetype feat, you gain a +1 status bonus to your attack rolls with one-handed firearms and one-handed melee weapons until the end of your turn.
name: Phenom's Verve

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reach For the Sky](/Feats.aspx?ID=8761) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 137

[Pistol Phenom Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8756)

**Requirements**
You’re wielding a loaded firearm.

</column>

---

You command the room, firing a gun into the air and ordering everyone to surrender and put their hands in the air. You fire your firearm and then attempt a single [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) each enemy within 30 feet. Creatures who become frightened instinctively put their hands in the air. If they had [Raised a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316), they lose the benefits, as they raise the shield above their head where it’s of little use to block attacks. With their hands in the air, each of these [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) creatures can’t use reactions or free actions that require their hands, or items held in their hands, until the beginning of their next turn. Regardless of the result, each creature is then temporarily immune to Reach for the Sky for 1 minute.
name: Reach For the Sky
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded firearm.

markdown:
**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 137 **Archetype** [Pistol Phenom](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=367) **Prerequisites** [Pistol Phenom Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8756) **Requirements** You’re wielding a loaded one-handed firearm and a one-handed melee weapon. --- You fire your gun while throwing off a target’s balance with your melee weapon. Then, you topple your foe to the ground with the quick orchestrated combination. You don’t need to use brute force to do so, or even necessarily to hit with your gunshot; the effect is all about performing perfectly and eliminating your target’s movements and options, leaving them no choice but to fall. Make a ranged firearm [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against a creature within the reach of your melee weapon and then attempt a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check against the creature’s Reflex DC. If the Strike critically succeeds, the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its Reflex DC against this check. The Performance check has the following effects. **Critical Success** The target falls and lands [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). It takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage from the fall.
**Success** The target falls and lands [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** Your plans backfired on you, and you’re forced to drop your melee weapon in your space.
name: Whirling Knockdown
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded one-handed firearm and a one-handed melee weapon.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sniping Duo Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8763) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 138

**Archetype** [Sniping Duo](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=368)

**Prerequisites**
trained in at least one type of weapon in the bow or firearm groups; Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

</column>

---

When you take this dedication, choose one willing, non-minion ally to act as your spotter. As part of your training in this archetype, you train your spotter in the necessary habits and techniques to use your abilities automatically; your spotter doesn’t need to spend any feats of their own to grant the effects. You only gain the benefits of your Sniping Duo archetype feats if both you and your spotter are alive and conscious. You and your spotter don’t grant your foes lesser [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) against one another’s Strikes. Whenever you or your spotter successfully Strikes a target, the other member of your duo gains a +1 circumstance bonus per weapon damage die on the damage roll of their next Strike made against that target before the end of their next turn.

You can change your designated spotter by spending 3 days of downtime training with another ally.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you’ve gained two other feats from the sniping duo archetype.

<title level="2">Sniping Duo Dedication leads to...</title>
[Concentrated Assault](/Feats.aspx?ID=8771), [Duo's Aim](/Feats.aspx?ID=8767), [Eagle Eyes](/Feats.aspx?ID=8770), [Exploit Opening](/Feats.aspx?ID=8765), [Tag Team](/Feats.aspx?ID=8769), [Targeted Redirection](/Feats.aspx?ID=8766), [Triangulate](/Feats.aspx?ID=8764), [Vantage Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=8768)
name: Sniping Duo Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: trained in at least one type of weapon in the bow or firearm groups; Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

    pfs="Standard"

[Triangulate](/Feats.aspx?ID=8764) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />

**Prerequisites**
[Sniping Duo Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8763)

</column>

---

Your spotter’s quick signals help you make even difficult shots seem simple. As long as both you and your spotter can see the target of your attacks, you don’t take a penalty for attacking within your weapon’s second range increment. The penalty you take for firing into further range increments is –3 for the third range increment, with an additional –2 penalty for each additional increment beyond the third (–5 for the fourth range increment, –7 for the fifth, and –9 for the sixth).
name: Triangulate

</column>

---

You aim a shot at your spotter, intending for your spotter to redirect that shot off their own weapon or shield to hit your foe from a better angle, and avoid your foe’s [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372). If your next action is to make a ranged [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167), you treat your spotter’s position as your own for the purposes of determining cover and range. Your spotter must be within your weapon’s first range increment and they must use a reaction to redirect your shot.
name: Targeted Redirection

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

</column>

---

With assistance from your spotter, you aim for an especially accurate attack. Make a ranged weapon [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against a foe either within your spotter’s melee reach or the first range increment of a ranged weapon your spotter is wielding. On this Strike, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the attack roll and ignore the target’s [concealment](/Rules.aspx?ID=424). If you’re using a firearm with the [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=847) trait, you don’t take the normal circumstance penalty to this Strike for not having the required Strength score or firing without using a tripod.
name: Duo's Aim

You and your spotter take advantage of each other’s attacks to momentarily hide and throw your foes off-balance against your follow-up attack. When you successfully make a ranged Strike against a foe, your spotter can use a reaction to attempt a Stealth check against the foe’s Perception DC. On a success, the foe is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the spotter’s next attack before the end of your spotter’s next turn. Similarly, when your spotter successfully makes a ranged Strike against a foe, you can use a reaction to attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) check against the foe’s Perception DC. On a success, the foe is flat-footed against your next attack against them before the end of your next turn.

</title>

<traits>

If you and your spotter both use the [Ready](/Actions.aspx?ID=2299) activity to make a Strike against the same opponent and choose the same trigger for the readied action, resolve your attacks at the same time. Each of you can use the higher of the two attack rolls for your Strikes, each using your own modifier; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect. If both attacks are successful, total the damage for both attacks for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

</traits>

<additional-info>
**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 139

**Prerequisites**
[Sniping Duo Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8763)

</additional-info>

---

<summary>
You and your spotter combine your efforts to bring down your target.
</summary>
summary_markdown: You and your spotter combine your efforts to bring down your target.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spellshot Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8772) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Class" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=397" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 140

**Archetype** [Spellshot](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=369)

**Prerequisites**
[way of the spellshot](/Ways.aspx?ID=11)

</column>

---

You cast arcane spells like a [wizard](/Classes.aspx?ID=39), gaining a spellbook with four common [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) [cantrips](/Rules.aspx?ID=2227) of your choice. You gain the [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) activity. You can prepare two cantrips each day from your spellbook. You’re trained in the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics. Your key spellcasting attribute for spellshot archetype spells is Intelligence, and they are arcane spells. You become trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35); if you were already trained in Arcana, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. This counts as the [wizard archetype](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=218) for the benefits of [Basic Wizard Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=5109).

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat other than Beast Gunner Dedication until you’ve gained two other feats from the spellshot or beast gunner archetypes.

<title level="2">Spellshot Dedication leads to...</title>
[Black Powder Embodiment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8776), [Call Gun](/Feats.aspx?ID=8774), [Fulminating Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=8773), [Phase Bullet](/Feats.aspx?ID=8775)
name: Spellshot Dedication

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fulminating Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=8773) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 141

**Archetype** [Spellshot](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=369)

**Frequency**
once per round.

**Prerequisites**
[Spellshot Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8772)

</column>

---

You channel potent magic into your next shot, empowering it with a blast of crackling energy. Choose [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604). If you hit with your next attack roll using a firearm or crossbow before the end of your turn, you deal 1d6 additional damage of this type. At 12th level, this increases to 2d6 additional damage, and at 18th level, it increases to 3d6 additional damage.
name: Fulminating Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Call Gun](/Feats.aspx?ID=8774) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 141

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The Spellshot archetype’s Call Gun action can only be used on a gun in the character’s possession._

**Archetypes** [Spellshot](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=369), [Beast Gunner](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=116) (Level 8), [Beast Gunner](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=363) (Level 8)

**Prerequisites**
[Spellshot Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8772)

</column>

---

You build a powerful magical connection with a chosen gun or crossbow, gaining the ability to conjure it directly to your hand from any distant location. During your daily preparations, choose a single crossbow or firearm. Until your next daily preparations, you can use the Call Gun action to call the gun to your hand. 

**Call Gun** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Effect** You hold aloft a free hand and call the firearm or crossbow you chose during your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575) into your hand. As long as the weapon you chose is on the same plane, it appears in your hand.
name: Call Gun

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Phase Bullet](/Feats.aspx?ID=8775) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 141

**Archetype** [Spellshot](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=369)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Spellshot Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8772)

</column>

---

You can aim your bullets not just in three normal dimensions, but in additional dimensions as well, allowing you to fire a magical shot that phases through everything but your target. Make a crossbow or firearm Strike against a foe who’s [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=84) or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) to you (but not [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96)). The ammunition travels to your target in a straight line, passing through any non‑magical barriers or walls in its way, though magical barriers and force effects stop the bullet. The shot ignores all cover, the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition, the hidden condition, and circumstance bonuses to AC from shields. It has a +4 status bonus to hit creatures wearing any type of armor. The Strike’s damage can’t be reduced with a [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) reaction using a non‑magical shield.
name: Phase Bullet

    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Black Powder Embodiment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8776) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />

**Archetype** [Spellshot](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=369)

**Prerequisites**
[Spellshot Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8772)

**Requirements**
You’re wielding a loaded magical firearm or crossbow.

</column>

---

You infuse your entire being into a single shot, merging your body into the blast and enabling you to travel the same path as your bolt or bullet. [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) a creature with the required weapon. On a success, if the target was within 120 feet, you and all your gear are teleported to an open space of your choice within 10 feet of the target.
name: Black Powder Embodiment
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a loaded magical firearm or crossbow.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unexpected Sharpshooter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8777) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 142

**Archetype** [Unexpected Sharpshooter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=370)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)

</column>

---

Who needs skill when you’ve got dumb luck? Sometimes your shots hit miraculously, regardless of the fact that you seemed to slip when you pulled the trigger or that you weren’t aiming at your foe when you fired. You gain the Accidental Shot activity.

**Accidental Shot** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You make a Strike with a ranged weapon, rolling the attack and damage twice and using the better results for each. The attack ignores circumstance penalties to the attack roll and any [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) required due to the target being [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79).

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you’ve gained two other feats from the unexpected sharpshooter archetype.

<title level="2">Unexpected Sharpshooter Dedication leads to...</title>
[Chain Reaction](/Feats.aspx?ID=8784), [I Meant to Do That](/Feats.aspx?ID=8783), [Lucky Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=8778), [No Hard Feelings](/Feats.aspx?ID=8779), [That Was a Close One, Huh?](/Feats.aspx?ID=8780), [Unbelievable Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=8782), [Unbelievable Luck](/Feats.aspx?ID=8781)
name: Unexpected Sharpshooter Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lucky Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=8778) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Misfortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=110" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 142

**Archetype** [Unexpected Sharpshooter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=370)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Unexpected Sharpshooter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8777)

**Trigger**
A creature targets you with an attack, even if you aren’t aware of it.

</column>

---

Your enemy lies in wait, lines up the perfect shot, and pulls the trigger... then at just the right moment you duck down to notice something scrawled on the cobblestone in chalk, a shiny coin, or some other coincidental distraction, creating an opportunity for the attack to miss. The attacker must roll the attack twice and use the worse result
name: Lucky Escape
